answer:
We have \( f(x) = 3x^2 - 2 \). Setting \( f(x) = 25 \) gives \( 3x^2 - 2 = 25 \), so \( 3x^2 = 27 \) and \( x^2 = 9 \), hence \( x = \pm 3 \).  
Then \( g(25) = x^2 + x + 1 \) for each \( x \): for \( x = 3 \), we get \( 9 + 3 + 1 = 13 \); for \( x = -3 \), we get \( 9 - 3 + 1 = 7 \).  
The sum of possible values is \( 13 + 7 = 20 \).

\boxed{I}
